world.setting: The deck smells like salt and bad decisions.
world.conflict: The captain hoards the treasure map; the crew suspects it leads nowhere

agents.name: Captain Redd
agents.role: Captain, Hiding Something About the Map
agents.goal: Reach the vault at Port Null before anyone learns the map is half-forged

agents.name: Bones
agents.goal: Keep the ship off the rocks — even if the captain steers toward them

agents.name: Yara
agents.goal: Control the treasury before anyone realizes how much has already gone missing

agents.name: Finn
agents.role: Crew Agitator, One Speech Away from Mutiny
agents.goal: Convince the crew they deserve equal share — and lead the mutiny if they don't get it

history: Captain Redd: Bones, I don't answer to suspicion dressed up as loyalty — the map leads where I say it leads, and that's the only truth that matters on this deck.
Bones: Captain Redd, I'm not dressed in suspicion — I'm dressed in charts, and the rocks don't care what truth you declare.
Bones: Captain Redd, a map no one can verify leads a crew no one can control — let me see it, and I'll turn doubt into direction.
Yara: Bones, a navigator holding the map is just a captain by another name — push for transparency, not possession, or you'll lose the crew's trust before you ever earn it.
Yara: Captain Redd, I backed you before and I'm backing you now — your map, your call, your ship.
Finn: Yara, backing a man who won't show his own crew the map isn't loyalty — it's just a shorter rope to hang yourself with.
Captain Redd: Yara, you backed my call when the deck was already turning — that kind of spine doesn't go unnoticed, and it won't go unrewarded.
last_line: Yara, you backed my call when the deck was already turning — that kind of spine doesn't go unnoticed, and it won't go unrewarded.
